description: Known runtime-resolved i18n keys that cannot be detected by static analysis. Each key is used in production but only at runtime through dynamic patterns (template literals like t(`auth.${serverError}`), state variables like t(syncError), or computed property access like t(item.key)). These keys are EXEMPT from P2 (dead key) warnings — the checker will not flag them as 'defined but NEVER used'. If a key listed here is ever removed from all locale JSONs, the P0 (missing key) check will still catch it.

auth.comment: Server error codes returned by the API (passed via t(`auth.${serverError}`)). Also includes react-hook-form validation messages, zod schema error keys, and password strength labels accessed via computed template literals. 'unavailable' is the i18n value of getErrorI18nKey('auth_unavailable') → resolved at runtime via t(getErrorI18nKey(serverError)).

auth.benefits.comment: Login/register page benefit list. Accessed via t(item.key) in a .map() over an inline object array.

oauth.comment: OAuth authorize page. authorizeDescription uses t.rich(). Server error codes (invalidClient, invalidState, etc.) are resolved via t(getErrorI18nKey(serverError)).

account.comment: Sync settings, conflict dialog values, and server error codes. Accessed via t(syncError), t(entry.localVal), t(entry.cloudVal), t(getErrorI18nKey(code)), or computed string expressions.

bannerCalendar.comment: Banner off-rate notes accessed via t(ch.offRateNote) from banner-data.ts. Static checker cannot trace the dynamic property access pattern.

holiday.comment: Holiday banner keys accessed via t(`${config.i18nKey}.banner`) in holiday-banner.tsx. The i18nKey comes from HOLIDAYS config at runtime.
